author: maxine--basel
content:
Root cause: .hub-write-lock is a runtime PID lock file (created/deleted by HubWriteLock RAII guard in sync/cache.rs) but it's tracked in the crosslink/hub git branch. Every sync cycle: (1) acquire_lock writes PID to file, (2) drop deletes it, (3) next clean_dirty_state() sees dirty state, (4) commits 'sync: auto-stage dirty hub state (recovery)', (5) can't push because of divergence, (6) repeat. This created 274 recovery commits in one crash cycle.

Fix: add .hub-write-lock to .gitignore on the hub branch and git rm --cached it. The lock file works fine untracked — it's OS-level file locking with PID liveness checks.

Affected code: crosslink/src/sync/cache.rs lines 41-52 (try_create_lock), lines 25-37 (Drop impl), lines 460-504 (clean_dirty_state).
